title: Stocks Face Rising Risk With Mega AI Deals Ready to Flood Market
summary: A flood of new shares from companies looking to fund their artificial intelligence ambitions is raising questions on Wall Street about whether there will be enough buyers to soak them all up and what this pile of fresh equity will mean for stock prices more broadly.

title: Etihad Airways CEO on Competition & Asian Market Growth
summary: Etihad Airways CEO Antonoaldo Neves speaks at the International Air Transport Association (IATA) on EU carrier advantages, growing Asian traffic, and competition. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Air New Zealand CEO on "Sky Nest" Beds
summary: Air New Zealand CEO Nikhil Ravishankar speaks at the International Air Transport Association (IATA) on "Sky Nest" bunk beds, a lie-flat sleeping pod which can be booked for four-hour sessions on long-haul flights. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: IAG CEO on Asia demand, Consolidation & Fuel Prices
summary: International Airlines Group (IAG) CEO Luis Gallego speaks at the International Air Transport Association (IATA) on growing demand for Asia, consolidation & increasing fuel prices. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bond Traders Bet on a CPI Surge That Bolsters Case for Fed Pivot
summary: Bond traders are wagering that inflation figures this week will show the biggest surge in consumer prices in several years, adding to pressure on the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates.

title: Air New Zealand CEO on Consolidation & Price Increases
summary: Air New Zealand CEO  Nikhil Ravishankar speaks at the International Air Transport Association (IATA) on flight consolidation and price increases to meet demand. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Embraer Arijan Meijer on Tariff Impact Within Aviation
summary: Embraer CEO Arijan Meijer speaks at the International Air Transport Association (IATA) on the impact of tariffs saying it "makes sense" for aviation to be excluded. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bloomberg This Weekend 6/07/2026
summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by Florida Rep. Greg Steube, Air France-KLM Benjamin Smith, United Airlines CEO Scott Kirby, Oklahoma Sen. Alan Armstrong, GE Aerospace Larry Culp, Puck News Washington Reporter Abby Livingston and Eagleton Center for Public Interest Polling Ashley Koning. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ECB Steps Up as G7’s Lead Hawk With Interest-Rate Hike Primed
summary: A euro-zone interest-rate hike in the coming week is set to place the European Central Bank at the vanguard of global tightening caused by the Iran war.

title: Etihad Airways CEO Antonoaldo Neves on Fuel Prices
summary: Etihad Airways CEO Antonoaldo Neves speaks at the International Air Transport Association (IATA) on fuel prices saying "things are coming back." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Brazil Budget Freeze Grounds Aircraft Approval Process
summary: Brazil’s decision to freeze part of the budget of its civil aviation regulator is threatening to delay the arrival of new aircraft and planned capacity expansions just as airlines race to meet robust travel demand and address fleet shortages.

title: The Innovative Digital Archives at the Obama Presidential Center
summary: Reflecting his status as a contemporary president, 95% of President Obama's records are digital. The decision was made to forgo the traditional physical archive and provide access to these digital files instead. Bloomberg New's Alexandra Lange joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the creation of the Obama Presidential Center.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ingredion Is in Advanced Talks to Acquire Tate & Lyle for £2.7 billion
summary: Ingredion Inc. is in advanced talks to acquire Tate & Lyle Plc in a deal valued at £2.7 billion ($3.6 billion), people with knowledge of the matter said, as another famous brand prepares to leaves the London stock market.

title: GE Aerospace Evolves Amid High Aviation Demand
summary: CEO of GE Aerospace Larry Culp spoke with Bloomberg's Co-Anchor of The Opening Trade Guy Johnson about GE's recent corporate restructuring and the company's current position and strategic outlook within the aerospace industry.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: OPEC+ Agrees Another Symbolic Quota Increase for July
summary: Major OPEC+ members agreed another modest symbolic increase to their oil output quotas for July, even as a blockage of exports from the Persian Gulf prevents most of them from implementing it.

title: Sen. Armstrong Advocates for Energy Infrastructure Expansion
summary: Senator Alan Armstrong recently resigned as the executive chairman of Williams Companies to replace Markwayne Mullin in the Senate. Armstrong joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the need for US infrastructure to meet the growing energy demand of AI data centers.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Rep. Steube Discusses Concerns Over Acting DNI Head
summary: President Trump has appointed Bill Pulte as the Acting Director of National Intelligence which has mixed reactions from lawmakers on both sides of the aisle. Republican Congressman Greg Steube joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss his opinion on Pulte's appointment. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: United Airlines CEO Discusses Industry Challenges
summary: At The International Air Transport Association in Rio De Janeiro, United Airlines CEO Scott Kirby discussed with Co-host of Bloomberg Surveillance Lisa Abramowicz the current challenges facing the airline industry including rising fuel costs and their impact on ticket pricing. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Can a Forgotten Caribbean Town Become a Spaceport?
summary: The Dominican Republic’s plan to build a commercial spaceport in the small town of Oviedo is about far more than rockets. Launch on Demand founder Burton Catledge says the project could create thousands of jobs, attract new investment, and help diversify the country beyond tourism. Supporters envision a new hub for aerospace, technology, and education, while local residents hope the project can bring opportunities to a region that has long felt overlooked. But before launches begin, developers must build the infrastructure, workforce, and regulatory framework needed to support an entirely new industry. For many in Oviedo, the spaceport represents a chance to put their town, and perhaps the country itself, on the map.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Air France-KLM CEO Discusses Resuming Gulf Flights
summary: Air France-KLM CEO Benjamin Smith discussed the airline's cautious approach to resuming flights in the Gulf region, confirming the return to Riyadh while other destinations like Dubai, Tel Aviv, and Beirut remain closed.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Worries Grow That Hedge Fund Crowding May Amplify Risk in Crisis
summary: Friday’s sudden equities rout after a months-long rally is renewing concerns that the unwinding of crowded trades could exacerbate market losses.

title: GE Sees Potential For More China Deals After Trump-Xi Meeting
summary: GE Aerospace remains optimistic that it can secure more aircraft-engine orders from China after President Donald Trump and his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping met in Beijing last month and manufacturing partner Boeing Co. came home an initial batch of commitments.

title: Africa Finance Corporation Samaila Zubairu Interview
summary: Africa Finance Corporation President and CEO Samaila Zubairu sits down with Bloomberg to discuss $2 billion syndicated loan. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Africa Finance Corporation CEO on Syndicated Loan
summary: Africa Finance Corporation President and CEO Samaila Zubairu speaks on raising $2 billion via a syndicated loan. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: FIFA World Cup Set to Be Most Lucrative Cup Amid US Hosting Concerns
summary: The upcoming FIFA World Cup, hosted across North America, is projected to generate a record $11 billion in revenue. However, there are some questions on the actual benefits for the United States playing host. Bloomberg Business of Sports Columnist Adam Minter came on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss what hosting such an event means for the host country.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Air France-KLM CEO on Fuel Prices and Gulf Flights
summary: Air France-KLM CEO Ben Smith speaks at the International Air Transport Association (IATA) saying "we'll make do" with the availability of fuel and the that he expects 5-10% of customers to return, when flights through the Gulf return to full capacity. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SpaceX IPO: What You Need to Know
summary: Ahead of SpaceX's highly anticipated IPO Co-host of Bloomberg Tech Ed Ludlow, Bloomberg New's Senior Equities reporter Bailey Lipschultz, and Bloomberg's Senior Aerospace Defense and Airline analyst George Ferguson join David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss market expectations and the potential impact on the industry. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: The Knicks Lead The Spurs 2-0
summary: The NBA Finals features two of the most marketable teams in the league. On one side of the finals matchup, the New York Knicks have their ties to NYC, the iconic MSG, and their legacy as one of the original NBA franchises. The other side of the court, the San Antonio Spurs are led by 22 year-old French superstar Victor Wembanyama a 7'4 player considered to be the future of the league. Host of Sports City and Co-host of Bloomberg's Business of Sports podcast Randall Williams joins Bloomberg This Weekend hosts David Gura and Christina Ruffini to discuss how the broader NBA landscape could benefit from the competitive series. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: EM Long Bonds Seen Missing Out on Any Iran Peace Dividend
summary: Emerging-market bond investors expecting a broad rally from any US-Iran peace deal may be in for a disappointment, as money managers see sticky inflation and fiscal concerns keeping long-term yields elevated.

title: OPEC+, Aviations Execs Address Iran War Impact at Duel Conferences
summary: The seven countries of OPEC+ hold their monthly meeting while airline executives gather for their annual conference, both covering the impact of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Co-Host of Bloomberg Surveillance Lisa Abramowicz and Bloomberg News Middle East Correspondent Abeer Abu Omar join David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the information that has come from these two events. (Source: Bloomberg)
